Sphera is a leading global provider of enterprise software and services that enables companies to manage and optimize their environmental, health, safety and sustainability. Our mission is to create a safer, more sustainable and productive world.

Sphera is a portfolio company of Blackstone, a U.S.-based alternative asset investment company that focuses on private equity, technology and innovation, and more. Blackstone businesses succeed through strong partnerships, a personalized approach and a commitment to exceptional performance with uncompromising integrity. Sphera and Blackstone are leaders in the Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) space.

We are guided by our core values of Customer Centricity, Accountability, Bias to Action, Innovation, and Collaboration. These values help us recruit the right talent to join our rapidly expanding team around the globe. It is important to us that each and every Spherion is not only eager to challenge themselves and knows how to get work done but is an awesome addition to our company culture.

The Solution Executive is responsible for driving new enterprise revenue by selling Spheraâs solutions to large, complex organizations within the Government sector, with a strong focus on US Federal and SLED Agencies and programs. This role requires sophisticated enterprise solution selling, strong executive presence, and disciplined deal execution when managing complex enterprise sales cycles.

The Solution Executive acts as a trusted advisor to C-suite and senior executives, helping organizations address complex business challenges, improve decision-making, and deliver measurable business value through technology solutions. The role requires the ability to engage senior stakeholders, understand strategic priorities, and position solutions that align with long-term business objectives.

Success in this role requires experience closing multi-year, six- and seven-figure enterprise software agreements using a structured qualification and forecasting methodology such as MEDDPICC.

What Youâll Do


Own and grow a defined set of enterprise accounts, with a strong focus on net-new logo acquisition
Execute against annual quota through disciplined pipeline generation, opportunity qualification, and deal execution
Lead C-suite and executive-level conversations centered on global regulatory compliance, product marketability, chemical risk, and sustainability obligations
Apply MEDDPICC to rigorously qualify opportunities, assess deal health, and drive predictable outcomes
Identify and articulate customer pain tied to regulatory complexity, non-compliance risk, product recalls, fines, restricted substance exposure, and delayed market access
Deliver an executive-level Sphera point of view on chemical intelligence and supply chain risk
Manage complex, multi-stakeholder buying cycles involving economic buyers, technical decision-makers, compliance leaders, IT, legal, and procurement
Collaborate cross-functionally with Solution Engineering, Presales, Product, Regulatory Content, Delivery, and Customer Care to advance deals
Maintain accurate pipeline, deal plans, and forecasts in Salesforce
Develop and deliver value-based solution presentations that clearly communicate business impact, risk reduction, and ROI
Lead government and partner negotiations and close enterprise software license agreements
Create and execute detailed closing plans aligned to customer buying processes


What Youâll Need


7+ years of experience selling enterprise SaaS or software solutions to large, complex organizations selling into the Public Sector market
Proven success selling to C-suite and senior executives
Demonstrated experience closing six- and seven-figure enterprise deals with long, consultative sales cycles
Experience selling into the US Public Sector
Strong command of MEDDPICC (or equivalent enterprise sales qualification methodology)
Consistent track record of meeting or exceeding annual quota
Highly driven, determined, and coachable, with the ability to quickly learn new industries, regulatory environments, and compliance frameworks
Ability to lead value-based discovery and articulate complex regulatory and compliance concepts in clear business and financial terms
Strong negotiation skills and experience navigating procurement and legal processes
Comfortable operating in a matrixed, cross-functional enterprise environment
Proficiency with Salesforce, LinkedIn, and standard enterprise sales tools
Entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to operate as the âCEO of your territoryâ


Pay

$105,000.00 - $167,000.00 + Eligible for Variable Compensation Plan

Benefits

Commensurate with relevant qualifications and experience

Health Savings Account
Flexible Spending Account
401(k) Retirement Plan with Company Match

rawText:
About SDL

GovPilot provides cloud-based software solutions to local governments, aiming to make our communities better places to live and work. As a mission-driven company, GovPilot is committed to creating impactful technology that serves the public good.

We are looking for team members who take pride in their work and seek to consistently improve themselves, their skills, and their knowledge. If this sounds like youâand the type of work environment where youâd thriveâwe hope youâll join us!

We offer flexible remote work options and are primarily based in the US East Coast time zone. You do not have to relocate to join GovPilot!

What We Offer


Competitive salary.
Unlimited PTO.
Flexible working hours and remote work options.
Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance.
Professional development opportunities and learning budget.
Home office and IT budget.
Supportive team environment.
The chance to work on innovative projects with cutting-edge technologies.


Job Summary

The Senior Account Executive will be responsible for driving new business revenue by developing and closing sales opportunities with local government agencies. This role requires demonstrated success in SaaS sales, preferably within the public sector, and the ability to manage complex procurement cycles. In addition, the Senior Account Executive will play a pivotal role in establishing GovPilot's market presence by securing flagship municipal clients in states where the company is building an initial footprint. The successful candidate will be highly motivated, results-oriented, and adept at cultivating trusted relationships with municipal stakeholders at all levels.

Key Responsibilities


Develop and maintain relationships with existing local government customers to drive engagement and product adoption.
Identify opportunities for upselling and cross-selling additional products and services to meet customer needs.
Conduct regular account reviews, analyze usage data, and provide strategic recommendations to optimize customer value.
Work closely with the customer success team to ensure a seamless customer experience and drive retention.
Partner with sales and marketing teams to develop tailored upsell and cross-sell campaigns.
Negotiate contract renewals, expansion and close new deals while ensuring customer satisfaction.
Stay up-to-date with industry trends, local government challenges, and competitive offerings.


Qualifications & Skills


Prior experience selling permitting software or related municipal government technology solutions is strongly preferred.
Ability to effectively manage complex, long-duration sales cycles.
3+ years of experience in account management, customer success, or sales, preferably in GovTech.
Proven track record of driving revenue growth through new business/logos and experience successfully upselling and cross-selling.
Strong understanding of local government operations and procurement processes is a plus.
Excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills.
Ability to analyze customer data and derive actionable insights.
Self-motivated, results-driven, and able to work both independently and collaboratively.
Proficiency in CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot) and data analytics platforms.

description:
About SDL

GovPilot provides cloud-based software solutions to local governments, aiming to make our communities better places to live and work. As a mission-driven company, GovPilot is committed to creating impactful technology that serves the public good.

We are looking for team members who take pride in their work and seek to consistently improve themselves, their skills, and their knowledge. If this sounds like youâand the type of work environment where youâd thriveâwe hope youâll join us!

We offer flexible remote work options and are primarily based in the US East Coast time zone. You do not have to relocate to join GovPilot!

What We Offer


Competitive salary.
Unlimited PTO.
Flexible working hours and remote work options.
Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance.
Professional development opportunities and learning budget.
Home office and IT budget.
Supportive team environment.
The chance to work on innovative projects with cutting-edge technologies.


Job Summary

The Senior Account Executive will be responsible for driving new business revenue by developing and closing sales opportunities with local government agencies. This role requires demonstrated success in SaaS sales, preferably within the public sector, and the ability to manage complex procurement cycles. In addition, the Senior Account Executive will play a pivotal role in establishing GovPilot's market presence by securing flagship municipal clients in states where the company is building an initial footprint. The successful candidate will be highly motivated, results-oriented, and adept at cultivating trusted relationships with municipal stakeholders at all levels.

Key Responsibilities


Develop and maintain relationships with existing local government customers to drive engagement and product adoption.
Identify opportunities for upselling and cross-selling additional products and services to meet customer needs.
Conduct regular account reviews, analyze usage data, and provide strategic recommendations to optimize customer value.
Work closely with the customer success team to ensure a seamless customer experience and drive retention.
Partner with sales and marketing teams to develop tailored upsell and cross-sell campaigns.
Negotiate contract renewals, expansion and close new deals while ensuring customer satisfaction.
Stay up-to-date with industry trends, local government challenges, and competitive offerings.


Qualifications & Skills


Prior experience selling permitting software or related municipal government technology solutions is strongly preferred.
Ability to effectively manage complex, long-duration sales cycles.
3+ years of experience in account management, customer success, or sales, preferably in GovTech.
Proven track record of driving revenue growth through new business/logos and experience successfully upselling and cross-selling.
Strong understanding of local government operations and procurement processes is a plus.
Excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills.
Ability to analyze customer data and derive actionable insights.
Self-motivated, results-driven, and able to work both independently and collaboratively.
Proficiency in CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot) and data analytics platforms.

rawText:
Senior Sales Executive - Public Sector
Worldpay (now part of Global Payments) â Atlanta, GA Â· Hybrid Â· Full-time

Job Description
Are you ready to write your next chapter? Make your mark at one of the biggest names in payments. We're looking for a Senior Sales Executive to join our ever-evolving Enterprise Public Sector team and help us unleash the potential of every business.

What You'll Own
As a Sr. Sales Executive, Public Sector, you will be charged with developing and managing an identified market segment that is strategic to Worldpay's long-term growth objectives. You will be involved in identifying new target segments, collecting information that would be of assistance in developing product solutions, and understanding the revenue possibilities available to Worldpay on an annual basis, forming partnerships with key influencers in your segment.

- Responsible for new account development
- Closing business with new accounts
- As an expert in the field, uses professional concepts in developing resolution to critical issues and broad design matters.
- Identify new client targets and key contact people within public sector, loading into Salesforce.com
- Consistently achieve quota and proactively manage opportunities to maintain appropriate mix of prospects at various stages of the sales cycle
- Sell to C-level executives within target segments

What You'll Bring
- As an ideal candidate you will have established relationships in the public sector vertical (state, local or federal government) and strong knowledge of payments processing
- Must have 7+ years of experience selling to financial decision makers within the Public Sector (CFO, Controller, Finance Manager, Director of Finance)
- You will have proven ability to sell business technology solutions to large enterprise companies
- Excellent presentation and writing skills
- Ability to thrive in a high-performance team
- Ability to travel up to 50%

Compensation
For this full-time position, the good faith estimated annual salary range upon hire is $118,300 - $175,975 base. Actual starting salary will be determined by experience, job-related skills, and relevant education or training.

About Worldpay
Worldpay combined with Global Payments in January 2026 to create a leading payments technology and software company that powers commerce for businesses of all sizes worldwide. Global Payments processes billions of transactions and trillions in annual payment volume across more than 175 countries. Headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, Global Payments is a Fortune 500 company and a member of the S&P 500.

Note: 4 University of Maryland alumni have been hired at Worldpay (per LinkedIn).

Account Executive - Southeast SLED
Glocomms (recruiter â confidential client) â Texas, United States Â· Remote Â· Full-time

About Our Client
Our client is the leading platform for digital identity verification and fraud prevention, leveraging AI and machine learning to help organizations confidently establish trust online. Their solutions are trusted by some of the largest public and private institutions to combat fraud, improve access, and streamline digital experiences.

Role Overview
Our client is seeking a seasoned SLED Account Executive to drive revenue growth across State, Local, and Education (SLED) customers in the Southeast region. This role owns the full enterprise sales lifecycle, selling a mission-critical SaaS platform into complex government environments.

The ideal candidate has deep experience closing large, 7-figure enterprise deals, is comfortable navigating public sector procurement, and brings a consultative, outcomes-focused approach. Prior experience selling identity verification or fraud prevention solutions is preferred but not required. Experience selling enterprise SaaS or cybersecurity solutions is required.

Key Responsibilities
- Own and execute a comprehensive territory plan for SLED accounts across the Southeast
- Build, develop, and manage a robust pipeline of qualified public sector opportunities
- Navigate complex, multi-stakeholder sales environments including IT, security, procurement, and executive leadership
- Lead full-cycle enterprise sales motions, including RFPs, contract negotiations, and multi-year agreements
- Successfully close large, complex deals, including transactions exceeding seven figures in total contract value
- Collaborate closely with Solutions Engineering, Customer Success, Marketing, and Product teams to deliver value-based solutions
- Develop trusted, long-term relationships with key decision makers and influencers
- Maintain accurate pipeline management, forecasting, and CRM hygiene
- Represent our client at industry events, conferences, and customer engagements

Required Qualifications
- 8+ years of enterprise sales experience, including significant exposure to SLED or public sector customers
- Demonstrated success closing 7-figure, complex SaaS or cybersecurity deals
- Experience managing long, consultative sales cycles with multiple stakeholders
- Strong understanding of government procurement processes, including RFPs, RFIs, and contracting
- Track record of consistently meeting or exceeding quota in high-value territories
- Exceptional communication, presentation, and negotiation skills at the executive level
- Willingness to travel as needed throughout the Southeast region

Preferred Qualifications
- Experience selling identity, fraud prevention, risk, or security-related solutions
- Established relationships within state, local, or education agencies in the Southeast
- Familiarity with modern cloud architectures and enterprise security platforms

Compensation: $150,000 - $175,000 base salary (OTE not disclosed). No visa sponsorship.

About Glocomms
Glocomms is a specialist technology recruitment firm founded in 2013. This posting is on behalf of a confidential identity-verification / fraud-prevention SaaS client.

Director of Sales, Strategic Accounts (Southeast)
Axon â Florida-Remote

Join Axon and be a Force for Good.
At Axon, we're on a mission to Protect Life. We're explorers, pursuing society's most critical safety and justice issues with our ecosystem of devices and cloud software. Like our products, we work better together. We connect with candor and care, seeking out diverse perspectives from our customers, communities and each other.

Life at Axon is fast-paced, challenging and meaningful. Here, you'll take ownership and drive real change. Constantly grow as you work hard for a mission that matters at a company where you matter.

Lead and Develop a High-Performing Sales Team. Directly lead a team of T1200 Account Executives covering Axon's most strategic State & Local Law Enforcement accounts. Recruit, onboard, and develop top sales talent; build a high-bar, accountable, performance culture.

Own Segment Strategy and Execution. Build and execute a comprehensive go-to-market plan for the T1200 segment aligned with Axon's strategic priorities.

Experience selling to public sector / SLED, ideally State & Local Law Enforcement or adjacent public safety markets.
Demonstrated ability to exceed multi-million-dollar bookings and revenue targets through team leadership.

Sales Manager, SLED
NinjaOne â Hybrid, Austin, TX

About the Role
NinjaOne is seeking an experienced Sales Manager to lead our SLED Sales team, with a focus on managing our SMB and Mid Market SLED Account Executives. This leadership role is critical in developing and scaling our go-to-market strategies for State & Local Governments and Education prospects. As the Sales Manager, SLED, you will guide a team of Account Executives who bring strong sales acumen and a passion for helping public sector organizations modernize and simplify IT.

What You'll be Doing
- Lead the SMB and Mid-Market SLED sales team to exceptional quota attainment.
- Hire, train, and manage a team of Account Executives focused on new logo and expansion revenue across State, Local, and Education accounts.
- Develop and execute sales strategies to penetrate SLED markets and guide your team to success.
- Coach team members through full-cycle sales motions, including prospecting, discovery, demo, proposal development, negotiation, and close.
- Coordinate, direct, and monitor the pipeline health of your team by tracking and reporting weekly activities and KPIs.
- Partner cross-functionally with marketing, channel, sales engineering, and executive leadership.
- Build consistency and discipline in forecast accuracy, pipeline generation, and deal execution.

About You
- 2 to 5 years of proven sales leadership experience.
- 5+ years of proven successful experience in a B2B sales role, ideally in a SaaS model focused on selling into SLED accounts.
- Consistent track record of overachievement against quota as an individual contributor.
- Proven track record of success managing a sales team.
- Strong understanding of SLED buying cycles, procurement processes, contract vehicles, and channel-aligned motions.
- A Salesforce enthusiast; experience with Salesloft, GovSpend, GovWin a plus.

About Us
NinjaOne unifies IT to simplify work for nearly 40,000 customers in 140+ countries. The NinjaOne Unified IT Operations Platform delivers endpoint management, autonomous patching, backup, and remote access in a single console. Hybrid remote, unlimited PTO, 401(k). NOT eligible for Visa sponsorship.

rawText:
Account Executive, Public Sector
Promise â US Remote; Oakland; Washington, D.C.

Company Overview
Promise modernizes how government agencies and utilities support people in financial difficulty. We build technology that makes it simple for residents to receive benefits, engage with assistance programs, set up flexible payment plans, and stay on trackâwhile helping agencies increase efficiency, recover revenue, and deliver services with dignity. Our team includes experts from Palantir, Google, Stripe, and government leaders. Backed by $50M+ from Reid Hoffman, Howard Schultz, Michael Seibel, Y Combinator, 8VC, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, XYZ Ventures and others. Recognized as one of Fast Company's "World's Most Innovative Companies of 2022," "Forbes Next Billion-Dollar Startups 2024," and Y Combinator's #1 GovTech startup.

About the Role
As an Account Executive, you will drive new and expansion business across targeted agencies and enterprises. Each customer has unique procurement processes, and successful AEs at Promise see these as opportunities to demonstrate creativity and value. You'll work closely with GTM leadership to shape outbound strategy, open new verticals, and build a repeatable motion as Promise expands nationwide.

What You'll Do
- Build and execute targeted account plans for government agencies and utilities.
- Navigate complex and slow procurement processes with patience and precision.
- Close new and expansion deals; establish and grow relationships with senior stakeholders across public sector and regulated enterprises.
- Respond to and win strategic RFPs.
- Deliver tailored demos and presentations that align Promise's products with each client's needs.
- Partner with GTM leadership to design outbound strategies, define personas, and refine messaging for new verticals.
- Deliver accurate forecasts and pipeline updates.
- Travel up to 50%.

What Will Enable You
- 5+ years of experience selling software into public sector agencies, utilities, or similarly complex regulated industries.
- Proven track record of building and closing new business in long-cycle enterprise sales environments.
- Strong network and ability to leverage senior advisors to generate outbound opportunities.
- Experience developing sales playbooks, writing prospecting copy, designing cadences.
- Metrics-driven with CRM/GTM tools (HubSpot, Salesforce, or equivalents).
- Demonstrated ability to navigate multifaceted procurement processes, from RFPs to enterprise contracts.
- Comfortable in an early-stage, fast-changing environment.

Hybrid: in-office or on-site at least four days a week. US persons only (citizens / permanent residents); must reside in US.

Compensation Range: $192K - $280K

Enterprise Account Manager - SLED
Agiloft
Remote â United States â Full-time

About Agiloft
Agiloft is the global value leader in data-first contract lifecycle management (CLM), offering the industry's only no-code platform with AI on the inside to enhance efficiency, cut review times by up to 80%, and accelerate business. Its Data-first Agreement Platform (DAP) transforms contracts into strategic, data-rich assets. Trusted by brands globally with a 96% renewal rate. KKR majority stake acquired 2024.

About the Role
This role is ideal for an experienced enterprise seller who thrives in complex public-sector environments, excels at consultative selling, and is comfortable discussing AI's strategic value in transforming contract management. You will manage and grow existing SLED accounts while identifying new expansion opportunities.

Key Responsibilities
- Develop and execute account strategies to expand Agiloft's footprint across assigned SLED customers
- Drive upsell and cross-sell opportunities within existing accounts
- Manage renewal cycles with a focus on customer value realization
- Build trusted relationships with stakeholders at all levels, including procurement, legal, finance, IT, and executive leadership
- Conduct strategic account reviews and success planning sessions
- Lead the end-to-end commercial process from opportunity identification to negotiation and close
- Navigate complex public-sector procurement cycles, including RFPs, RFIs, and cooperative purchasing agreements
- Maintain accurate forecasts, opportunity data, and account plans in Salesforce

Required Qualifications
- 7+ years of SaaS or enterprise software sales experience, preferably in the SLED or public-sector verticals
- Proven track record of managing and growing strategic enterprise accounts
- Familiarity with SLED procurement processes and contracting requirements
- Consultative selling approach with executive presence
- Strong Salesforce CRM proficiency
- Experience with AI, automation, or contract management concepts (preferred)

Compensation
OTE estimated at $280,000 - $340,000 (base $131K-$147K + variable); equity participation

As part of the Cloudera Government Solutions (CGSI) Sales team, this Account Executive will focus on selling to State and Local Governments, Higher Education, and Healthcare organizations. - Strong executive presence and relationship-building skills
- Territory planning and strategic account management capability
- Familiarity with government data privacy and security requirements

Compensation
OTE approximately $320,000 (base ~$155K median); comprehensive benefits

Senior Account Executive, Public Sector
Atlassian â Washington, DC Â· Remote Â· Full-time

Overview
Atlassians can choose where they work â in an office, from home, or a combination. Atlassian can hire people in any country where it has a legal entity.

Responsibilities
As a Public Sector Enterprise Advocate you are responsible for deeply understanding your customers and how they are leveraging Atlassian's suite of products. You will be nurturing and growing existing relationships and working diligently to build new ones. Through strategic account planning, client management, and consistent demonstration of value creation, you will drive the execution of our goals â most notably our customers' migration to our FedRAMP cloud offering.

You will be the customer account lead, setting direction and guiding orchestration for all support and cross-functional teams (Channel Partners, Solutions Engineers, and more). You will be a critical liaison between Atlassian's product/engineering executives and customers to help guide future roadmap and improve customer experience. Reports directly to the Director of Federal Sales.

Qualifications
- 8+ years experience of Federal software sales experience, focusing on strategic account management
- Strong relationships with Government Agencies, Solutions Partners, and Resellers
- Deep knowledge of Government contracts and procurement vehicles
- Experience with achieving success in customer-first SaaS organizations
- You excel when engaging directly with enterprise customers with a consultative, relationship-oriented approach
- Experience using CRM, Pipeline Management, and Analytic tools
- Someone who wants to challenge the traditional Sales Model and improve sales processes
- Loves working cross-departmentally, takes initiative, advocates for the public sector business
- Experience working with and advising C-level customers and stakeholders to drive outcomes

It Would Be Great If You Also Had
- Experience transitioning customers from traditional "on premise" deployment to FedRAMP certified SaaS solutions
- Entrepreneurial spirit combined with experience navigating a larger, matrixed organization
- Background in some or all of Atlassian's products
- Experience selling DevSecOps or ITSM solutions

Compensation
US geographic pay zones â Zone A: $166,500 - $217,375; Zone B: $149,400 - $195,050; Zone C: $137,700 - $179,775 base. Also eligible for benefits, bonuses, commissions, and equity.

About Atlassian
Atlassian powers the collaboration that helps teams accomplish what would otherwise be impossible alone. SLED Growth Account Manager
Skillsoft (NYSE: SKIL) â United States Â· Remote Â· Full-time

At Skillsoft, we believe skills fuel growth. Skillsoft is hiring an experienced State, Local, and Higher Education (SLED) Account Manager to lead account renewals and growth with a process-oriented mentality and a strategic, growth mindset, with a solution-selling approach. You will create renewal plays that accentuate Skillsoft's strengths in alignment with each client's training/development initiatives. This individual will have a track record of exceeding quota and will secure a 95%+ renewal rate while growing an upgrade pipeline. You must stay 90-120 days ahead of renewals and 2-3 quarters ahead in the sales cycle for upgrades, and be familiar with State, Local Government and Education buying cycles, fiscal periods, and contracting vehicles.

Responsibilities & Opportunity Highlights
- Drive revenue growth by navigating customers (renewal space) and enhancing existing customer relationships through upgrades
- Position Skillsoft's solutions to key decision-makers
- Collaborate with cross-functional reps to identify and pursue cross-sell opportunities
- Partner with cross-departmental reps and leaders to drive upsell using a land-and-expand approach
- Engage and influence senior leadership prospects to drive decision-making and close deals
- Take ownership of meeting preparation, including research, agendas, tailored presentations, follow-up
- Provide management with ongoing feedback on pipeline health, risks, opportunities, and progress

Required Skills & Qualifications
- 2-5 years of professional SLED sales experience preferred, ideally in PaaS, SaaS, B2B, and/or Safety/Legal compliance software, with a Bachelor's degree or equivalent
- Solid understanding of selling into State, Local & Higher Education Contracts
- Proficient in Salesforce and MS Office Suite
- Must reside in Central or East Time Zones to support East Regional clients; travel up to 10-20%
- Advanced knowledge and usage of Salesforce.com for pipeline management and reporting
- Expertise in negotiating deals based on value
- Familiarity with MEDDPIC or similar methodologies (desirable)

Compensation
Target base salary range approximately $55,000 to $62,500 annualized, with average on-target earnings including commissions of approximately $110,000 - $125,000 annualized. Enterprise benefits (medical, dental, vision, PTO).

About Skillsoft
Skillsoft (NYSE: SKIL) is a global leader in AI-native skills management. It unifies learning, real-time skills intelligence, and workforce insights, trusted by 60% of the Fortune 1000, supporting 105M+ learners. ~3,158 employees. Note: Skillsoft has hired 5 people from University of Maryland (per LinkedIn).

Regional Sales Manager - SLEd
Netskope â Florida, United States Â· Full-time

About Netskope
Today, there's more data and users outside the enterprise than inside, causing the network perimeter as we know it to dissolve. We realized a new perimeter was needed, one that is built in the cloud and follows and protects data wherever it goes, so we started Netskope to redefine Cloud, Network and Data Security.

Since 2012, we have built the market-leading cloud security company and an award-winning culture powered by hundreds of employees spread across offices in Santa Clara, St. Louis, Bangalore, London, Paris, Melbourne, Taipei, and Tokyo.

About the position
The Netskope Regional Sales Manager - SLEd Southeast will come on board with the full support of the executive team. This is an amazing opportunity for the sales professional who has a history of completely dominating their territory and who wants to make an impact on building the next iconic cloud security company. You are an expert at mapping business solutions to the most complex security challenges of customers. You understand the competition and have the desire to win market share.

Responsibilities include:
- Prospect new accounts, generate interest, qualify, develop, and close new business
- Work independently to meet and exceed revenue targets and goals assigned to the territory
- Develop an overall account strategy leading to the deployment of a well-executed selling effort into the assigned territory/accounts
- Focus on new accounts, customer satisfaction, and retention

Job Requirements:
- 10+ years of a successful track record selling cybersecurity and networking technologies including network security technologies such as Proxies, Next Generation Firewalls (NGFW), SSL/IPSec, VPN's, SSO, DLP and Encryption gateways and growing a territory
- Exceptional track record of success with consultative sales of complex enterprise software solutions to C-level executives in F1000 accounts
- Verifiable track record of exceeding quotas year after year
- Resides in the targeted geography w/local enterprise customer relationships
- Experience establishing and fostering strong Channel Partner relationships. Pre-existing relationships with key resellers in the assigned territory is a must
- Understanding of enterprise web technologies and SaaS experience a must
- Travel: within region

Education: Bachelor Degree preferred

About Netskope
Today, there's more data and users outside the enterprise than inside, causing the network perimeter as we know it to dissolve. We realized a new perimeter was needed, one that is built in the cloud and follows and protects data wherever it goes, so we started Netskope to redefine Cloud, Network and Data Security.

Since 2012, we have built the market-leading cloud security company and an award-winning culture powered by hundreds of employees spread across offices in Santa Clara, St. Louis, Bangalore, London, Paris, Melbourne, Taipei, and Tokyo.

About the position
The Netskope Regional Sales Manager - SLEd Southeast will come on board with the full support of the executive team. This is an amazing opportunity for the sales professional who has a history of completely dominating their territory and who wants to make an impact on building the next iconic cloud security company. You are an expert at mapping business solutions to the most complex security challenges of customers. You understand the competition and have the desire to win market share.

Responsibilities include:
- Prospect new accounts, generate interest, qualify, develop, and close new business
- Work independently to meet and exceed revenue targets and goals assigned to the territory
- Develop an overall account strategy leading to the deployment of a well-executed selling effort into the assigned territory/accounts
- Focus on new accounts, customer satisfaction, and retention

Job Requirements:
- 10+ years of a successful track record selling cybersecurity and networking technologies including network security technologies such as Proxies, Next Generation Firewalls (NGFW), SSL/IPSec, VPN's, SSO, DLP and Encryption gateways and growing a territory
- Exceptional track record of success with consultative sales of complex enterprise software solutions to C-level executives in F1000 accounts
- Verifiable track record of exceeding quotas year after year
- Resides in the targeted geography w/local enterprise customer relationships
- Experience establishing and fostering strong Channel Partner relationships. Pre-existing relationships with key resellers in the assigned territory is a must
- Understanding of enterprise web technologies and SaaS experience a must
- Travel: within region

Education: Bachelor Degree preferred

Director, Public Sector Sales
SteadyIQ â Remote Â· Full-time
Reports to: Chief Commercial Officer Â· Travel: 30â40%

About SteadyIQ
SteadyIQ is transforming how eligibility verification works for public benefits programs. Building on roots in income verification, SteadyIQ has evolved into a broader platform to support seamless, unified verification experiences across programs, data sources, and user journeys. Our secure, user-consented approach enables agencies to access accurate, real-time information â reducing administrative burden, improving program integrity, and creating a more intuitive experience for the people they serve.

The Role
SteadyIQ is seeking a Director, Public Sector Sales to originate, advance, and close new business with state and local agencies while supporting long-term account growth. This is a full-cycle, quota-carrying role responsible for building pipeline, leading sales engagements, navigating procurement, and closing deals. Individual contributor position with no immediate direct reports.

You will serve as a primary external face of SteadyIQ â engaging state leaders, demonstrating the platform, and guiding opportunities from initial interest through contract execution and long-term growth, all documented in HubSpot CRM.

What You'll Own
- Pipeline Development & Market Engagement â proactive outreach, conferences/events, executive engagement
- Full-Cycle Sales Execution â own from initial engagement through contract execution, including proposal scoping, stakeholder alignment, and negotiation
- Procurement & RFP Leadership â lead/coordinate RFP, RFI, and procurement vehicle responses; navigate state procurement timelines
- Account Growth, Renewal & Expansion â support renewal/expansion in existing state accounts
- Go-To-Market Collaboration â partner with marketing, product, customer experience to refine messaging and inform product strategy

Qualifications
- 7â12 years of experience in public sector sales, govtech, consulting, or related fields
- Strong understanding of state government procurement processes and contracting vehicles
- Experience engaging with state or local agencies, preferably in health and human services or public benefits programs (HHS, Social Services)
- Ability to independently conduct product demos and communicate SaaS solutions effectively
- Proven ability to build relationships and close new business opportunities
- Comfort operating in a fast-paced, startup environment with a high degree of autonomy
- Willingness to travel frequently (30â40%)
- Experience closing high-value, complex SaaS/technology deals in the public sector

Total Compensation Package
- Base Salary: $140,000 â $175,000
- Variable: Uncapped commission structure for new business and renewals
- Eligible for equity
- 100% company-paid medical/dental/vision for employees and families
- Unlimited PTO

NetDocuments â United States Â· Remote Â· Full-time

NetDocuments is the world's #1 trusted cloud-based content management and productivity platform that helps legal professionals do their best work. Named to Inc. 5000 (2022, 2023, 2024); multi-time Top Workplaces winner.

What You'll Do
We are seeking a Senior Enterprise Account Executive â Public Sector to drive new business and expansion within state & local government and U.S. federal agencies. This is a high-impact, quota-carrying role focused on complex, multi-stakeholder SaaS deals, with average deal sizes in the six-figure range. You will own the full sales cycle for a defined territory, selling the NetDocuments platform (including FedRAMP-authorized offerings) into legal, IT, records, and line-of-business stakeholders.

You will:
- Own and execute a territory plan across State & Local (SLED) and Federal accounts
- Build a predictable pipeline of six-figure opportunities
- Lead complex sales cycles from discovery through close, navigating procurement, legal, and security
- Conduct deep discovery on document, email, and records management needs (FOIA, case management, discovery, retention, compliance)
- Develop ROI/TCO analyses and business cases tailored to executives, business sponsors, legal teams, and IT/security
- Navigate public sector contract vehicles and procurement pathways (e.g., Carahsoft/TD SYNNEX Public Sector)

- Build multi-threaded relationships across agencies (legal leadership, CIO/CTO/CISO, records managers, program owners)

What You'll Need
- 7+ years enterprise SaaS / software sales, with 3â5+ years selling into public sector (SLED and/or Federal)

- Demonstrated success selling content management, document management, collaboration, legal tech, records management, or adjacent platforms
- Experience selling FedRAMP, StateRAMP, or similarly security-sensitive SaaS
- Strong consultative selling; value-based business cases and ROI analyses
- Excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills
- Ability to travel within the U.S.

Stand-out qualifiers
- Selling into legal teams (Attorney General, Corporation Counsel, GC, USAO)
- Familiarity with Microsoft 365 ecosystem
- Knowledge of FOIA, public records management, government records retention
- Familiarity with Carahsoft, NASA SEWP, ITESSW2, OMNIA Partners

Compensation
$130,000 - $150,000 base + Variable; equity not specified. 90% company-covered medical, HSA contribution, 401K match at 4% (immediate vesting), flexible PTO (3â4 weeks), 10 paid holidays.

We're hiring a Sales Director, Public Sector / Growth to build and own government revenue from the ground up across state, local, and public-safety agencies. This is a foundational leadership role for a senior seller who thrives in RFP-heavy environments, enjoys building process from scratch, and wants real ownership â not just a book of business.

You'll take full responsibility for the public-sector motion: shaping pre-RFP strategy, running complex procurements end-to-end, expanding into new states, and helping define what "great" looks like as the company scales. The right person will combine hands-on execution today with leadership mindset for what comes next. Reports directly to executive leadership and has meaningful influence over long-term GTM strategy, team structure, and market expansion.

What You'll Do
- Own revenue and pipeline across state, county, law-enforcement, justice, and social-services agencies
- Lead RFPs end-to-end: qualification, positioning, cross-functional coordination, narrative development, compliance, demos, negotiation, and contracting
- Manage deal sizes ranging from ~$10K to $1M+ ACV, including multi-year agreements
- Run proactive pre-RFP motions: stakeholder mapping, early influence, positioning ahead of major procurements
- Build territory strategy down to the agency, certification, and contract-vehicle level
- Create scalable, repeatable RFP processes, templates, and AI-assisted workflows
- Partner closely with product, engineering, operations, and legal to align offerings with public-sector requirements
- Help design and grow the future public-sector team, including recommending and hiring RFP/technical writing support
- Support adjacent enterprise or partnership initiatives as needed

What You Bring
- Proven success selling into public-sector or government environments
- Deep hands-on experience owning RFP-driven sales motions from start to finish
- Comfort operating in both high-velocity and long-cycle sales environments
- Strong executive presence and credibility with senior government stakeholders
- High judgment and integrity; experience working with external partners such as lobbyists is a plus
- Builder mentality â excited to create process, not inherit it
- Excellent written communication; fluency with AI-assisted research and drafting tools
- Bonus: experience in biometric identity, background checks, public safety technology, or other compliance-heavy industries

Benefits include 15 days PTO year one (20 thereafter), public holidays, medical/dental/vision, onsite fitness facility access for local employees.

rawText:
Enterprise Account Executive, SLED
Flexera â United States Â· Remote Â· Full-time

The Enterprise Account Executive will sell the Flexera One platform and other Flexera core on-prem solutions to a set of State, Local and Higher Education accounts in the US. This position will play a crucial role in our company's growth strategy by targeting and securing new and expanding public sector accounts where Flexera has no existing footprint as well as maintaining and nurturing existing customer relationships.

You will be responsible for meeting and exceeding annual quota through execution of prospecting sales strategies and accurate forecasting. You will meet/exceed revenue goals by partnering with a team of Solution Engineers, Business Development, Channel, Marketing, and other members of the Flexera organization.

Responsibilities:
- Prospect across a broad range of accounts utilizing various lead generation techniques to uncover new business opportunities and cultivate a robust pipeline
- Establish trust and credibility with prospective clients by demonstrating a deep understanding of their business challenges
- Conducting comprehensive needs assessments to identify pain points, challenges, and objectives
- Articulate Flexera's solutions to precisely address the unique needs of each potential client
- Foster and maintain robust relationships with key stakeholders within target accounts, leveraging MEDDPIC methodology
- Collaborate effectively with the Pre-sales team to develop and deliver impactful presentations and product demonstrations
- Work in tandem with Channel & Alliance representatives on joint opportunities
- Accurately forecast opportunities based upon realistic assessments and deliver against that forecast
- Address customer objections and concerns effectively providing solutions and building trust
- Navigate and overcome barriers related to migration, cost, security, and performance
- Successfully negotiate to achieve favorable pricing and contractual agreements

Qualifications & Experience:
- 6+ years of experience selling Enterprise Software solutions to State, Local and Higher Education accounts
- Experience developing relationships with Public Sector Partners and System Integrators
- Experience utilizing a defined Sales Methodology (MEDDPICC, MEDDICC, Challenger, etc.)
- Successful in finding and uncovering opportunities with net new and existing accounts via creative prospecting tactics
- Full ownership of the end-to-end sales process (not an overlay role)
- Strong reputation for exceeding sales quota
- Consultative sales experience targeting relevant companies/businesses to drive cost savings and efficiencies throughout their IT organization
- Highly motivated and professional, with excellent verbal communication, presentation, and social skills
- Bachelor's degree preferred or equivalent experience

Featured benefits: Medical, Vision, Dental insurance; 401(k); Paid maternity/paternity leave.

About Flexera
Flexera helps organizations understand and maximize the value of their technology, saving billions of dollars in wasted spend. Powered by the Flexera Technology Intelligence Platform, our award-winning IT asset management, FinOps and SaaS management solutions provide comprehensive visibility and actionable insights on an organization's entire IT ecosystem. More than 50,000 global organizations rely on Flexera. ~2,072 employees globally. Note: 4 University of Maryland alumni at Flexera per LinkedIn.

rawText:
Account Executive, State & Local Government Sales
Dun & Bradstreet â United States Â· Remote Â· Full-time

The Public Sector Job Family is responsible for selling the Company's products and services to existing clients and developing or expanding business within the Local and Federal Governments that are of significant strategic importance to the Company and will have a major impact on the Company's long-term success. Maintain and grow the revenue stream through renewal management, win-back, cross-sell, up-sell of new opportunities. Identify new contacts and deepen relationships with current contacts. Generate new business through new client identification.

Essential Key Responsibilities
- Build strong relationships at senior levels and throughout assigned Government clients
- Build and maintain ongoing strategic Executive level customer relationships across the Agency's/Departments
- Leverage knowledge of Agency Missions to introduce D&B core capabilities and create opportunities
- Lead account planning activities with team leader and other sales support team members
- Meet and exceed overall sales target through the retention and growth of assigned accounts
- Measure and communicate progress against monthly targets by accurately and timely reporting/forecasting
- Maintain consistent and accurate data in SFDC to support territory, account planning and forecasting

Education and Experience
- Bachelor's degree required with 12 to 15 years of relevant experience
- Master's degree preferred

Essential Skills and/or Certifications
- Minimum of 12 years prior experience in a government and/or enterprise level SaaS, consulting or services sales role
- Impressive track record of closing sales, winning clients, managing client relationships and attaining or exceeding annual quota(s)
- Ability to rapidly assess client environments from a business process, organizational and technological perspective
- Demonstrable track record in managing complex government sales and managing multiple senior stakeholders
- Highly articulate with excellent business communication and presentation skills
- Possesses excellent industry-leading sales methodology, salesforce.com, MS-Excel, MS-PowerPoint and MS-Word skills
- Willing to travel beyond city limits for the interest of business

Featured benefits: Medical, Vision, Dental insurance; 401(k); Paid maternity/paternity leave.

About Dun & Bradstreet
Dun & Bradstreet (NYSE: DNB) is a leading global provider of business decisioning data and analytics. Since 1841, companies of every size have relied on D&B to manage risk and reveal opportunity. ~5,764 employees globally. Note: 10 University of Maryland alumni at D&B per LinkedIn.

rawText:
Public Sector Account Executive
Findhelp â United States Â· Remote Â· Full-time

We're changing the way people connect to social care.
At Findhelp, we've built a comprehensive platform of products and services that make it easy to connect people to resources, follow them on their journey, and track impact in a fast and reliable way. Our industry-leading social care network includes more than half a million local, state, and national programs that serve every ZIP Code in the country.

Findhelp is headquartered in Austin, Texas and has been enabling healthcare, government, education, and other organizations to connect people with the social care resources that serve them, with privacy and security, since 2010.

Job Overview
The Public Sector Account Executive will use prospecting best practices to generate new opportunities and move them forward in the sales cycle. This person will carry a quota in an assigned territory. The Public Sector Account Executive must be a strong communicator, presenter, and demonstrate an ability to collaborate with many different internal and external stakeholders for each opportunity. Experience selling to government entities including state departments and counties is a requirement.

Responsibilities and Duties
- Own all customer development activities and quota achievement in a named territory
- Create plans and strategies for developing new opportunities and delivering on sales targets
- Develop and maintain relationships with all stakeholders on target accounts within the territory
- Use the CRM to accurately forecast opportunities and to regularly update and manage a pipeline
- Collaborate on campaigns, events and other activities in support of customer development goals
- Expertly deliver compelling and customized product demonstrations of the Findhelp platform
- Provide continuous feedback to the broader team about product positioning, feature requests, and competitive landscape
- Be a key voice in sales planning, prioritization setting, and process optimization

Qualifications
- BA/BS degree or similar college level education; in lieu of degree, relevant skills or equivalent experience
- 3-7 years of experience selling to government entities including state departments and counties
- Proof of consistent achievement or overachievement of sales quota
- Experience prospecting, building and managing a sales pipeline, forecasting and reporting
- Strong collaboration and relationship management skills
- Ability to communicate value proposition for SaaS technology

Perks include: 401k & equity grants, paid parental leave, competitive PTO & 10 paid holidays, comprehensive medical/dental/vision (100% company-paid employee-only), 24/7 telemedicine.

Recently secured $250M funding from TPG's The Rise Funds. Competes with Aunt Bertha, Unite Us, Healthify. ~248 employees. Note: 1 University of Maryland alum at Findhelp per LinkedIn.

Streetleaf is modernizing outdoor infrastructure with solar-powered, smart lighting and technology solutions that reduce costs, improve safety, and meet sustainability goals. Public Sector Strategic Account Executive - DoD - GitLab

About GitLab:
GitLab is an open core software company that develops the GitLab DevSecOps platform. GitLab's platform helps teams collaborate on code, automate testing, security scanning, and deployment. GitLab is the most comprehensive AI-powered DevSecOps platform, used by over 30 million registered users and deployed at scale in government agencies.

About the Role:
GitLab is hiring for a Strategic Account Leader to help Department of Defense agencies modernize how they build and secure software using GitLab's AI-powered DevSecOps platform. This role is part of GitLab's rapidly growing Public Sector team.

Responsibilities:
- Own a defined book of business within the Department of Defense
- Build and execute multi-year account plans for DoD agencies
- Navigate complex procurement cycles including DoD contracting vehicles
- Develop relationships with DoD program managers, CIOs, and technical leadership
- Work with Solutions Architects and Customer Success to drive adoption

Requirements:
- 7+ years enterprise software sales experience with DoD or national security focus
- Experience navigating DoD procurement and acquisition processes
- Track record in complex, multi-stakeholder enterprise sales
- Understanding of DevSecOps, cybersecurity, or software development lifecycle
- Active or prior security clearance preferred (not always required)

Compensation: OTE estimated $200,000 - $280,000

Location: Remote, United States

Account Executive, Risk/Fraud - DC/VA/MD
Thomson Reuters — Washington, DC · Remote (territory: DC/VA/MD)

This position is responsible for developing and executing account plans for new and/or existing small to mid-sized corporate accounts in an assigned territory. Expands revenue footprint with existing customers and prospects new business in order to close full solution sale to corporate customers.

Products: Risk/Fraud — https://legal.thomsonreuters.com/en/risk-fraud-investigations
Territory: DC, VA, MD (must live in territory)

About the Role
- Prospecting: Actively seek out new business with new and existing customers; keep pipeline 3x sales-target coverage monthly/quarterly
- Account Management: Companies with revenues less than $500M, full sales cycle prospecting to close to renewal
- Sales Goals: Meet/exceed revenue targets
- Cross-functional Collaboration: Work with other teams to tailor solutions
- Relationship Building: Establish/maintain executive relationships
- Salesforce maintenance and accurate forecasting
- Direct client meetings in person or via MS Teams

About You
- College degree preferred with a minimum of 4 years direct field sales experience (corporate-sector experience preferred) with proven sales-quota over-achievement
- Experience in FinTech and/or selling data solutions is a plus
- Proven ability to sell complex technology solutions to small/medium companies (<$500M revenue), using consultative and value-based approach
- High-velocity sales and land-and-expand experience; selling to C-level executives applying solution selling
- Skilled in leading detailed sales processes involving various stakeholders
- Enthusiastic about prospecting; manage entire cycle independently
- Ability to develop and execute an account plan
- Able to work from home office and travel to customer locations

Compensation
Target total cash compensation range for US locations: $136,500 - $253,500 (base + target sales incentive).

About Thomson Reuters
Thomson Reuters brings together trusted content and technology for legal, tax, accounting, compliance, government, and media professionals. 26,000 employees across 70+ countries. Reuters, part of TR, is a world-leading provider of trusted journalism.

rawText:
Enterprise Account Executive
Panorama Education — United States, Remote · Full-time

About the Role
Looking for an Account Executive to join the Enterprise revenue team. This role leads new sales and upsell efforts within a regional portfolio of school districts serving 20,000 or more students. You'll make meaningful relationships with strategic district and state education administrators, learning about their most pressing initiatives, and compel these leaders to partner with Panorama in support of their work.

Job Responsibilities
- Manage a sales territory and assigned quota within a portfolio of school districts (20,000+ students)
- Develop and execute account-based selling approach in the Enterprise segment
- Maintain a small portfolio of Panorama's largest accounts for annual renewal and cross-sell
- Confidently present to strategic leaders regularly — in-person meetings, conferences — on Panorama's fit for the largest K-12 school systems
- Establish and maintain relationships with strategic district and state education administrators
- Collaborate within revenue team and across Technology and Customer Experience to develop effective sales strategies
- Develop and maintain a deep understanding of Panorama's tech education solutions
- Maintain a well-organized sales and relationship pipeline
- Provide regular reports on sales activities

Compensation
On Target Earnings Range: $287,500 - $347,500 (base + commission)
Base Salary Range: $135,000 - $165,000 with variable target of $150,000

Ideal Candidate Has
- 7+ years in a closing role in Enterprise Sales, with education and full-funnel experience preferred
- Proven track record of hitting/exceeding revenue goals through Enterprise deals
- Ability to travel approximately 50%
- Experience leading effective presentations to strategic educational leaders virtually and in-person
- Experience preparing client-facing materials (proposals, email)
- Experience supporting existing accounts with a consultative approach
- Strong organizational/time-management skills

Bonus Points
- Worked in district or state leadership in K12 and have acute understanding of educator challenges
- Maintain existing set of relationships with educational leaders and buyers

Benefits include 401K with employer match, health/dental/vision/life/STD/LTD, FSA, Wellness Reimbursement, WFH Reimbursement, flexible vacation, parental leave.

rawText:
Senior Account Executive - SLED
Ultimate Knowledge International (UKI) — United States, Remote · Full-time

We are seeking an experienced and driven Senior Account Executive – SLED to lead enterprise sales efforts within the State, Local Government, and Education markets. This role is responsible for developing strategic relationships, managing complex procurement cycles, and driving revenue growth through consultative sales of enterprise solutions.

The ideal candidate brings 3+ years of direct SLED sales experience, understands government and education purchasing processes, and has a proven track record of navigating enterprise-level deals from prospecting through close.

Key Responsibilities
- Develop and execute strategic sales plans focused on State, Local Government, and Education accounts
- Build and maintain relationships with procurement teams, purchasing departments, IT leadership, fleet/operations stakeholders, and executive decision-makers
- Manage complex enterprise sales cycles including RFPs, RFQs, cooperative purchasing agreements, and contract negotiations
- Identify opportunities for new business growth while expanding existing customer relationships
- Understand customer operational needs and position solutions that align with procurement requirements
- Collaborate cross-functionally with marketing, customer success, operations, and leadership
- Maintain accurate forecasting, pipeline management, and CRM activity tracking
- Attend industry conferences, trade shows, and networking events relevant to SLED markets
- Stay informed on government procurement trends, compliance requirements, and purchasing vehicles

Requirements
- 3+ years of direct SLED sales experience in enterprise or technology-focused environments
- Proven success managing complex enterprise sales opportunities and achieving quota attainment
- Strong understanding of government and education procurement processes, cooperative purchasing contracts and purchasing vehicles, RFP/RFQ response processes, and enterprise account management
- Experience selling into state agencies, local municipalities, K-12 or Higher Education institutions
- Excellent presentation, negotiation, and communication skills
- Ability to build trusted advisor relationships with executive stakeholders
- Experience using CRM platforms such as Salesforce, HubSpot, or similar tools

Preferred
- Experience in SaaS, technology, fleet, public sector, or enterprise solution sales
- Existing network within SLED organizations and procurement channels
- Familiarity with NASPO, Sourcewell, OMNIA, BuyBoard, or other cooperative purchasing programs
- Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, Public Administration, or related field

Why Work With UKI
At Ultimate Knowledge, you'll join a team that's redefining cyber training through technical innovation, creativity, and mission focus. Medical/dental/vision (100% individual premium covered), STD/LTD, basic life, 401k with safe-harbor matching (up to 4% match with 5% contribution), 15 days PTO, 12 company holidays + Christmas Eve, professional training/certification support, fully remote work environment.

rawText:
Sales and Partnerships, Public Sector
Brain Co. — Remote (Flexible, with travel required for government partners and key engagements) · Full-time
Department: Partnerships

About The Role
We're hiring for our Government Partnerships team to drive our public sector expansion. This is a quota-carrying, externally facing role responsible for taking government opportunities from intro to qualification to deal to signed partnership. This is a quota-carrying, revenue-driving role with full ownership of the deal lifecycle. You will work directly with senior leadership and operate at the front lines of bringing Brain Co.'s AI products into state, local, and federal government environments.

This is NOT a policy role. This is NOT a "government advisor" role. This is a commercial role for someone who can convert complex, multi-stakeholder government opportunities into signed partnerships.

What You'll Do
- Own the full lifecycle of government opportunities: intro → qualification → stakeholder navigation → close → early relationship ownership
- Own a revenue target tied to closing Signed Design Partnerships (SDPs) and converting them into paid deployments
- Build, manage, and advance a pipeline of public sector opportunities from early signal through close
- Convert warm inbound / network-driven opportunities into Signed Design Partnerships (SDPs)
- Navigate complex government buying processes across agencies, departments, and stakeholders
- Drive momentum in ambiguous environments where there is interest but no clear owner
- Build relationships across city/state agencies, mayoral offices, permitting/infrastructure departments, and public sector ecosystems
- Partner closely with product & engineering to translate customer needs into deployable solutions
- Operate as a hybrid: seller (close deals), operator (move things forward without structure), and early account owner (maintain momentum post-close)

What Success Looks Like
This is a high-agency role where outcomes matter more than activity. Success is measured by:
- Consistently hitting or exceeding revenue targets tied to partnerships and deployments
- Maintaining a healthy, self-generated pipeline in addition to generating outbound and converting inbound opportunities
- Ability to generate and close Signed Design Partnerships (SDPs)
- Driving opportunities from early interest → committed pilot
- Creating momentum across complex, slow-moving government systems
- Owning relationships end-to-end without heavy internal support

Who You Are
- 4+ years of quota-carrying sales or partnerships experience
- Proven track record of closing complex, multi-stakeholder deals
- Experience selling into government or government-adjacent environments
- Ability to independently drive opportunities forward in ambiguous settings
- GovTech or public sector SaaS background
- Enterprise sellers covering SLED / public sector
- Startup sellers navigating complex buyer environments
- Partnerships leaders who have owned commercial outcomes

Why This Role Matters
Government systems are some of the most important and most outdated infrastructure in society. Brain Co. is already proving that AI can accelerate permitting, modernize public systems, and unlock billions in value. This role is how that actually happens. You are the person who turns interest → action, conversations → partnerships, and pilots → real deployments.

Location: Flexible, with travel required for government partners and key engagements.

rawText:
Named Account Manager, SLED
Palo Alto Networks

Your Career
The Named Account Manager (SLED) is a significant driver of company revenue and growth. As an experienced and dynamic sales professional, you're responsible for leading and driving sales engagements. You're motivated by the desire to solve critical challenges facing our customer's secure environment, so you're prepared to connect them with a solution for every stage of threat prevention. You'll be responsible for meeting and exceeding your quota by crafting and implementing strategic territory plans targeting deployments of the Palo Alto Networks Next Generation Security Platform.

Your Impact
As a Named Account Manager, you will drive and orchestrate complex sales cycles and work with your internal partners and teams to best serve the customer. You'll create and implement strategic account plans focused on attaining enterprise-wide deployments, understanding the strategic competitive landscape and customer needs so you can effectively position Palo Alto Networks in the Tallahassee, FL state government marketplace.

Responsibilities
- Drive and orchestrate complex sales cycles across SLED accounts in the Tallahassee, FL area (Florida state government hub)
- Create and implement strategic account plans focused on attaining enterprise-wide deployments
- Understand the strategic competitive landscape and customer needs to effectively position Palo Alto Networks Next Generation Security Platform
- Meet and exceed quota through crafting and implementing strategic territory plans
- Build and cultivate relationships with channel partners for a channel-centric go-to-market approach
- Lead all aspects of the sales cycle with the ability to uncover, qualify, develop, and close new, white-space territories and accounts
- Leverage five pillars of demand generation: AE prospecting, SDR, Marketing, Channel, and Customer referrals

Requirements
- 10+ years of SLED/public sector sales experience in Florida
- Experience covering State and Local Government accounts in the Tallahassee, FL area
- Strong track record selling complex, long-cycle enterprise software solutions
- Experience with channel-centric go-to-market approach leveraging channel partners
- SaaS-based architecture knowledge, ideally in networking and/or security context
- Ability to lead all aspects of the sales cycle from qualification through close
- Consultative selling skills with C-suite / CISO-level stakeholder engagement
- BS/BA degree or equivalent work experience

Compensation
- Starting base salary + commission target: $264,000 – $363,000/YR
- Restricted stock units (RSUs) and bonus also available

description:
Named Account Manager, SLED - North Florida (Tampa)
Palo Alto Networks

Your Career
The Named Account Manager (SLED) is a significant driver of company revenue and growth. As an experienced and dynamic sales professional, you're responsible for leading and driving sales engagements across Northern Florida (Tampa, Jacksonville, Orlando), with a particular focus on the education sector (Higher Education and K-12 school districts). You'll be responsible for meeting and exceeding your quota by crafting and implementing strategic territory plans targeting deployments of the Palo Alto Networks Next Generation Security Platform.

Your Impact
As a Named Account Manager for North Florida, you will drive and orchestrate complex sales cycles across SLED accounts — primarily education institutions and state/local government agencies. You'll create and implement strategic account plans focused on attaining enterprise-wide deployments, understanding how to win by using Channel partners with a channel-centric go-to-market approach.

Responsibilities
- Drive sales engagements across SLED accounts in Northern Florida: Tampa, Jacksonville, and Orlando
- Focus on the education sector: Higher Education institutions and K-12 school districts
- Create and implement strategic account plans focused on enterprise-wide deployments of PANW security platform
- Win and grow accounts using channel-centric go-to-market approach with key channel partners
- Meet and exceed quota through strategic territory planning and execution
- Build C-level / CISO relationships across state agencies, universities, and school districts

Requirements
- 10+ years of SLED sales experience covering Northern Florida (Tampa, Jacksonville, Orlando)
- Strong experience and focus on education sector: Higher Education and K-12
- Understanding of how to win by leveraging Channel partners with a channel-centric GTM approach
- Track record of success in complex enterprise technology sales
- SaaS-based architecture knowledge, ideally in networking and/or security context
- Strong consultative selling skills with executive-level stakeholder management
- BS/BA degree or equivalent work experience

Compensation
- Starting base salary + commission target: $264,000 – $363,000/YR (estimated, same level as Tallahassee role)
- Restricted stock units (RSUs) and bonus also available

companyOverview: Palo Alto Networks is the world's leading cybersecurity company, protecting over 80,000 customers across 150+ countries. Founded in 2005 by Nir Zuk, the company delivers an AI-powered integrated security platform including next-generation firewalls (Strata), cloud security (Prisma), endpoint and extended detection/response (Cortex XDR), and managed threat intelligence (Unit 42). PANW has been a consistent Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader across multiple cybersecurity categories. The company has FedRAMP-authorized and StateRAMP-certified products, with a growing SLED presence including school districts and universities transitioning to zero-trust network architecture.

About the role (reconstructed)
Strategic Account Executive at Pindrop is a senior, quota-carrying enterprise AE role responsible for driving new business and expansion across Pindrop's largest enterprise customers. Pindrop AEs sell voice authentication, anti-fraud, and deepfake/spoof detection into banks, insurance companies, government agencies, and large contact centers. The Strategic AE manages complex, multi-stakeholder sales cycles involving Fraud, Security, IT, Contact Center Operations, and Compliance buyers.

Likely responsibilities
- Own a portfolio of strategic enterprise accounts and a quota tied to new logo + expansion ARR
- Build executive relationships with Chief Fraud Officers, CISOs, Heads of Contact Center, Heads of Identity & Authentication
- Manage complex, long-cycle enterprise sales motions including RFPs, proof-of-value engagements, and multi-stakeholder evaluations
- Articulate Pindrop's voice/audio fraud prevention and deepfake detection value proposition
- Partner with SE, Customer Success, and Channel teams (Genesys, NICE, Cisco, AWS Connect, etc.)
- Forecast accurately in Salesforce and maintain pipeline rigor

Likely qualifications
- 8+ years of enterprise SaaS / security / fraud-prevention sales experience
- Track record of consistent quota over-achievement in $250K-$1M+ ACV deal sizes
- Strong relationships with Fraud, Identity, Contact Center, or CISO buyer centers
- Experience selling into financial services, insurance, healthcare, government call centers, or large retail
- Salesforce CRM mastery; MEDDPICC / Challenger fluency

Pindrop is the leader in voice authentication, anti-fraud, and deepfake / spoof detection for call centers and digital channels. Customers include 8 of the top 10 US banks, leading insurers, and contact-center operators. Headquartered in Atlanta, GA. Backed by Andreessen Horowitz, Citi Ventures, GV, IVP, Vitruvian Partners, and others.
